_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, aggregating prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and indexed to 2015 with a base of 100, currently stands at 132. This represents a 32% increase over the 2015 baseline. The price rise has been uneven across components and has escalated in recent years rather than gradually. This index reflects global commodity price movements from sources such as FRED and the IMF.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged the most, rising 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 22%, wheat at 7%, and sugar at 5%. These changes are largely attributed to weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening affecting orange juice and frost/drought impacting arabica coffee growing regions—rather than generalized inflation. The soft commodities involved trade in relatively thin markets, which can amplify price swings due to supply shocks.
notes.why: The headline increase in the basket is primarily driven by a few weather-sensitive commodities. Because these soft commodity markets are vulnerable to environmental factors, elevated prices can persist for multiple years depending on crop recovery and conditions. This suggests ongoing volatility but does not reflect broad-based inflationary pressures.
